beats.activity: Wake up, tend rooftop herb garden despite drizzle, quick breakfast
beats.reasoning: Eli wakes early to check on basil and tomatoes before rain intensifies; routine-bound and risk-averse, he'll want to secure plants. Yesterday's energy was 50/100 and satisfied—he's maintaining momentum with his valued daily ritual.

beats.activity: Drive to U-District clinic via surface streets (SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon)
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure forces a detour; risk-averse Eli leaves early to avoid the snarl and uses surface roads through Fremont/Wallingford, arriving well before 9am closure takes effect.

beats.activity: Veterinary technician work at clinic: pre-op checks, patient care, assist with procedures
beats.location_name: University District Animal Clinic (University District, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Full morning and early afternoon shift; steady clinic work with anxious animals and post-op monitoring—the meditative focus Eli values. Rain outside keeps him focused indoors.

beats.activity: Lunch at Solstice Wood Fired Pizza, grab a sandwich to avoid crowds
beats.location_name: Solstice Wood Fired Pizza (University District, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Familiar spot from yesterday; rain continuing through 2pm means indoor meal. Eli grabs quick food without lingering—he prefers focus and close friends over crowds, so he skips the lingering World Cup energy.

beats.activity: Continue afternoon clinic shift: patient intake, post-op monitoring, closing tasks
beats.location_name: University District Animal Clinic (University District, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block; rain ends around 2pm so the clinic rhythm continues steady. Eli finishes his shift by early evening.

beats.activity: Drive home from U-District via I-5 South and I-90 East (SR-520 closure now lifted)
beats.reasoning: Evening return commute after 6pm; SR-520 closure has ended, so Eli can take his normal route home. Drizzle is done by now; drive feels clear.

beats.activity: Quick dinner: reheat leftovers, eat at home
beats.reasoning: Consistent with yesterday's simple dinner routine; Eli is tired after a full day at the clinic and wants to settle in quickly.

beats.activity: Spend evening at pottery wheel: hand-building and glazing, podcast playing
beats.reasoning: Valued free-time activity; similar to yesterday's clay work. Routine-bound Eli uses pottery to decompress after a steady clinic day, letting his mind wander while focused on craft.
8+ hour sleep block, ending well before 06:00 next day. Eli is satisfied and tired from a productive day; he heads to bed with a clear conscience.

diary: Woke up to that soft grey drizzle and honestly, it felt right for a Tuesday. Spent a few minutes on the roof garden anyway—the basil doesn't care about weather. Got to the clinic early enough to miss the worst of the SR-520 mess, thank god. The morning was steady: pre-op checks, a nervous golden retriever, the usual rhythm. Grabbed a sandwich at Solstice around lunch instead of dealing with the crowd. Afternoon flew by—post-op monitoring on a cat spay, intake paperwork. By the time I left, the rain had stopped and the bridge was open again. Home by three-thirty. Reheated some pasta, then spent the evening at the wheel. My hands needed it more than my stomach needed a proper dinner. There's something about centering clay when your mind's been scattered all day. Felt my shoulders finally drop around six.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure actually worked in my favor—I left early enough to sidestep the worst congestion, which set a calm tone for the whole shift. Work was steady but not overwhelming, and I chose Solstice over the busier lunch spot because I needed quiet. By afternoon I was feeling a little detached from the routine, so I knew I needed the pottery wheel more than sleep. The clay is always honest feedback; my centering was sloppy at first, which told me I was still carrying tension. By the time I glazed that bowl, I'd metabolized the day into something creative. That's become my real ritual.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
